target_idiom: กลืนไม่เข้าคายไม่ออก
source_meaning: to be caught between the devil and the deep blue sea, to have to choose between a rock and a hard place
target_meaning: to feel inconvenient or awkward due to inability to make any choice or decision.

target_idiom: การส่งไม้ต่อ
source_meaning: splinters, wreck; together with the adverb tuusan as fortifier used in two expressions
target_meaning: abstract noun of ส่งไม้ต่อ (sòng-máai-dtɔ̀ɔ)

target_idiom: กาวใจ
source_meaning: bridge-builder (one who establishes links or friendly relations)
target_meaning: reconciliator; mediator

target_idiom: กิ่งทองใบหยก
source_meaning: Two people or things that seem extremely alike; two peas in a pod.
target_meaning: ideal combination; well-matched couple; perfect pair.

target_idiom: เกาะชายกระโปรง
source_meaning: to live hand-to-mouth, live paycheck to paycheck
target_meaning: to depend on a woman for livelihood.

target_idiom: แก่แดด
source_meaning: ahead of time (prior to the expected time; early)
target_meaning: precocious.

target_idiom: ไก่เขี่ย
target_meaning: chicken-scratch.

target_idiom: ข้าวยากหมากแพง
target_meaning: dearth: scarcity and dearness of food.

target_idiom: ข้าวแดงแกงร้อน
target_meaning: favour; kindness; benevolence; support.

target_idiom: เข้าโรง
source_meaning: to a particular place, into being on location or on site
target_meaning: to be in theatres; to start screening in theatres

target_idiom: ครูบาอาจารย์
target_meaning: teacher.

target_idiom: ความวัวไม่ทันหาย ความควายเข้ามาแทรก
source_meaning: to rain cats and dogs (to rain very heavily)
target_meaning: when it rains, it pours.

target_idiom: ใจเพชร
target_meaning: stout-hearted; strong-minded; unyielding.

target_idiom: ญาติดี
source_meaning: to be on good terms with someone
target_meaning: to befriend; to be friendly (to); to be on good terms (with); to have good relations (with); to be at peace (with).

target_idiom: ดาวรุ่ง
target_meaning: rising star

target_idiom: ตีปลาหน้าไซ
target_meaning: to ruin an imminent or forthcoming success of another person.

target_idiom: ถอยหลังเข้าคลอง
target_meaning: to retrogress; to regress; to retrograde.

target_idiom: ทองไม่รู้ร้อน
target_meaning: indifferent person; nonchalant person; person who cares not or pretends not to care.

target_idiom: ทำนาบนหลังคน
source_meaning: to line one's pockets, feather one's nest
target_meaning: to derive benefit from other's suffering; to make money from other's suffering; to enrich oneself at other's expense; to make money by oppressive means.

target_idiom: นกไร้ไม้โหด
source_meaning: Not relevant anymore, often used of past offenses or sorrows, similar in tone to English "let bygones be bygones".
target_meaning: person who is no longer in an influential or dependable position and is thus disregarded.

target_idiom: น้ำไหลไฟดับ
target_meaning: a great deal; incessantly; nonstop.

target_idiom: บั่นทอน
target_meaning: to discourage; to dispirit; to dishearten.

target_idiom: ใบไม้ร่วง
source_meaning: to be the end of the world (be catastrophic or devastating)
target_meaning: used to describe a great many deaths.

target_idiom: ปลาซิวปลาสร้อย
source_meaning: an insignificant detail, just one thing among many
target_meaning: minnow: comparatively small or insignificant person.

target_idiom: ปากคนยาวกว่าปากกา
target_meaning: gossip is louder than earshot.

target_idiom: ปากกาคมกว่าดาบ
source_meaning: to put pen to paper (start writing)
target_meaning: the pen is mightier than the sword.

target_idiom: ปากสว่าง
source_meaning: tongue, as in something to be loosened to represent someone starting to be very chatty
target_meaning: loose-lipped: given to indiscreet talk.

target_idiom: ป้ายสี
target_meaning: to slander; to smear; to sully, vilify, or soil a good name, reputation, etc.

target_idiom: ปิดทองหลังพระ
source_meaning: to hide one's light under a bushel (to conceal one's talents or positive qualities, especially due to modesty or shyness; to avoid attention)
target_meaning: to do good deeds without seeking attention or praise.

target_idiom: ปีกกล้าขาแข็ง
source_meaning: to stand on one's own two feet
target_meaning: to be no longer needing to rely on someone else for help or support; to be capable of supporting or depending on oneself.

target_idiom: เป็นปี่เป็นขลุ่ย
source_meaning: with one voice, in unison, together, simultaneously (of voices, uttered in the same time)
target_meaning: harmoniously, in united fashion

target_idiom: ผิดเป็นครู
source_meaning: to learn the hard way, learn one's lesson
target_meaning: Blunders are lessons. / Experience is the mother of wisdom.

target_idiom: ผีซ้ำกรรมซัด
target_meaning: misfortunes never come singly.

target_idiom: เพชรตัดเพชร
source_meaning: to beat someone at their own game
target_meaning: equal match in wit; fight or competition between equally smart people.

target_idiom: เพื่อนตาย
target_meaning: true friend.

target_idiom: แพะรับบาป
target_meaning: scapegoat

target_idiom: ฟางเส้นสุดท้าย
target_meaning: last straw.

target_idiom: มีอันจะกิน
target_meaning: comfortably off; well-to-do.

target_idiom: มืดฟ้ามัวดิน
target_meaning: in a very large number.

target_idiom: ยอมงอ ไม่ยอมหัก
target_meaning: better bend than break.

target_idiom: ไม้หลักปักขี้ควาย
source_meaning: to shilly-shally, tergiversate, procrastinate, vacillate, hem and haw, hum and haw
target_meaning: wavering person; irresolute person; easily influenced person.

target_idiom: ร้าวฉาน
source_meaning: to be stretched (of limits or restrictions)
target_meaning: ruptured; breached; broken.

target_idiom: ลงถนน
target_meaning: to take to the streets (for a protest or demonstration).

target_idiom: ลม ๆ แล้ง ๆ
target_meaning: not likely to come true, not likely to happen; fantastic, irrational, groundless; futile, useless.

target_idiom: เล็กพริกขี้หนู
target_meaning: small, tiny, or young, but surprisingly able, clever, wise, smart, harsh, severe, poisonous, or capable of causing widespread or serious destruction or impact.

target_idiom: เล่นกับหมา หมาเลียปาก
target_meaning: alternative form of เล่นกับหมาหมาเลียปาก (lên-gàp-mǎa-mǎa-liia-bpàak)

target_idiom: เล่นหูเล่นตา
source_meaning: to give someone the eye, make eyes at (to show flirtatious signs with one's eyes)
target_meaning: to flirt with the eyes; to make eyes at.

target_idiom: วัวสันหลังหวะ
target_meaning: person who is frightened or haunted by his own misdeed; person who has done something wrong and suffers from the guilty conscience.

target_idiom: ส่งไม้ต่อ
source_meaning: to pass the torch [ allative ] (transfer responsibility)
target_meaning: to let someone do or be something as one's successor; to pass (a role, position, office, etc) to someone; to pass (on) the torch.

target_idiom: สร้างภาพ
source_meaning: to try to get attention towards, make a scene of; (of negative things) blow out of proportion; (of self) toot one's own horn
target_meaning: to create, publish, or promote a good image (of, about, etc); to propagandise (a good point of); to pretend, to act, or to put on an act (to make oneself, someone, or something look good); to be pretentious, fake, insincere, ingenuine, or hypocritic.

target_idiom: สองจิตสองใจ
target_meaning: in two minds; hesitantly.

target_idiom: สองต่อสอง
source_meaning: quietly, without much fanfare (of doing some action as quietly as possible without making any unnecessary noise, especially when to do something surreptitiously)
target_meaning: privately; in private; alone; without anyone else involved.

target_idiom: สะเทือนขวัญ
target_meaning: shocking; appalling.

target_idiom: เสน่ห์ปลายจวัก
target_meaning: cooking talent.

target_idiom: ใส่บาตร
source_meaning: to put food on the table (provide enough money to cover basic necessities)
target_meaning: to offer food (to a priest) by placing the food into his alms bowl

target_idiom: หน้าอินทร์หน้าพรหม
target_meaning: authority figure; influential figure; powerful figure.

target_idiom: หนูตกถังข้าวสาร
target_meaning: poor man who gets a wealthy wife; poor man who marries into a wealthy family; man who marries a woman that has much higher financial status.

target_idiom: อดเปรี้ยวไว้กินหวาน
source_meaning: To wait for something very anxiously, to the point of desperation.
target_meaning: To refrain from doing or enjoying something in order to await a better thing; delay gratification to get something better in the future.

target_idiom: ออกร้าน
target_meaning: to open a booth or stall (at an exhibition, festival, fair, or the like).

target_idiom: อมพระมาพูด
source_meaning: to swear on a stack of Bibles, swear on the Bible
target_meaning: to support one's own speech by alluding to a sacred thing.

target_idiom: ออกหน้าออกตา
target_meaning: openly; in an open manner; without reservation or concealment.

target_idiom: เอามะพร้าวห้าวไปขายสวน
source_meaning: to show one's place, to teach someone a lesson, to make eat crow [ allative]
target_meaning: to show off one's knowledge to someone who is more knowledgeable.
